title: Courgette and Tomato Frittata
steps: Turn the grill on to preheat to a medium-high temperature.
Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan. Add the courgette and tomatoes and cook on the hob for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring often, until soft. Spread out over the base of the frying pan.
Beat the eggs and milk together and pour into the frying pan. Cook over a medium-low heat for 4 or 5 minutes to set the base, then transfer to the grill to set the surface – about 3 minutes.
Remove from the heat and let the frittata cool for 3 or 4 minutes.
Toast the slices of bread. Slice the frittata into wedges and serve with the toast.

title: Baked Potatoes with Mince
ingredients: 1 handful cup or button mushrooms, finely chopped
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C, fan 180°C, gas mark 6.
Prick potatoes all over with a fork before baking for 1 hour, or until tender.
Meanwhile, heat a large saucepan and add the mince, a handful at a time, cooking until browned.
Add the onion and garlic, cook for 1 minute, then add the mushrooms, stock and tomato purée. Stir well until hot, then simmer, part-covered, for 20 to 25 minutes.
Once everything's ready, split the baked potatoes open and divide the filling between them. Serve with the lettuce, onion and tomato side salad.

title: Brilliant Beef Curry
ingredients: 240g lean braising steak, cut into chunks
steps: Brown the beef in a large saucepan or flameproof casserole dish over a low heat.
Add the onion and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the peppers, carrots and garlic. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often.
Add the curry powder and stir well, then add the tomatoes, mango chutney, 300ml water and the tomato purée. Cover and simmer gently for 90 minutes to 2 hours.
About 15 minutes before serving, cook the rice according to packet instructions. Serve with the curry.

title: Breakfast Burritos
ingredients: 1 pepper, any colour, deseeded and finely chopped
steps: Lay out the tortillas on a work surface. Preheat the grill.
Beat the eggs and milk together in a bowl. In another bowl, mix together the tomatoes, spring onions and pepper.
Heat half a teaspoon of vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan and pour in a quarter of the beaten egg mixture. Cook on the hob over a medium heat for a few moments to set the base.
Layer 1 tortilla with the cooked egg, a quarter of the tomato mixture and 10g of cheese. Grill to further set the egg and melt the cheese.
Roll up the tortilla and slice in half. Repeat to make the other 3 tortillas.

title: Breakfast Fruit Salad
ingredients: Half a melon (cantaloupe or galia), deseeded and chopped
steps: Mix together the sliced kiwi fruit, grapes and melon in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, mix together the low-fat yoghurt and ginger (if using).
Share the fruit between 4 serving bowls and spoon the yoghurt mixture on top.

title: Cheat's Pizza Calzone
ingredients: 1 red pepper, deseeded and thinly sliced
1 yellow pepper, deseeded and thinly sliced
steps: Preheat the grill.
Arrange the peppers and mushrooms on a baking sheet and grill them for 4 to 5 minutes, turning once.
Add the tomatoes and herbs, then season with black pepper. Keep warm.
Put a tortilla into a dry frying pan and sprinkle a quarter of the cheese over the top. Cook over a medium heat for about 30 to 40 seconds until melted.
Add a quarter of the vegetable mixture to one side of the tortilla, then fold it in half. Cook for a few moments, then slide onto a warm serving plate.
Repeat with the remaining tortillas. Serve with the salad leaves, grated carrot and celery.

title: Cheese and Tomato Grilled Fish
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high. Grease a baking sheet with the vegetable oil.
Arrange the fish fillets on the baking sheet and spread 1 tablespoon of the tomato purée over each one. Top with the tomatoes, season and scatter the cheese on top.
Grill for 6 to 8 minutes, until the fish is cooked. Serve with green vegetables and cooked rice or boiled potatoes.

title: Pasta Vegetable Cheese Wedges
ingredients: 100g pasta shapes (or 300g cooked potato, in chunks)
steps: Cook the pasta shapes in boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan and gently cook the onion for 3 to 4 minutes. Remove from the heat and add the courgette, carrot and pasta. Mix well.
Beat the eggs and add the cheese. Stir in the dried herbs and season with pepper.
Pour the egg mixture into the frying pan with the pasta and vegetables. Cook over a low heat for 4 to 5 minutes to set the base. Preheat the grill to medium-high.
Put the frying pan under the grill and cook for 4 to 5 minutes until golden brown.
Leave to cool for a minute, then cut into 4 slices and serve with vegetables or salad.

title: Chicken and Vegetable Parcels
steps: Cut 4 pieces of greaseproof paper each measuring approximately 30cm square. Put each chicken breast onto a separate square.
Mix the vegetables together and place an equal amount on top of each piece of chicken. Sprinkle with 5-spice powder, ginger, soy sauce and pepper.
Fold up the paper to wrap up the chicken completely. Put the parcels into a steamer, cover and steam for 35 to 40 minutes.
Check that the chicken is properly cooked by inserting a sharp knife into the thickest part — the juices should run clear.
Serve the chicken in the parcels, along with vegetables and noodles, rice or potatoes.

title: Chicken and Tomato Jacket Potato
steps: Scrub the potatoes, then dry and prick each one several times with a sharp knife.
Oven method: Rub a few drops of olive oil into the potato skin and bake at 200°C (fan 180°C, gas mark 6) for 1 hour 15 minutes, or until tender.
When the potatoes are almost ready, cut the chicken breasts into bite-size chunks and pan fry for about 6 to 8 minutes until cooked through. Add a pinch of dried oregano.
Chop the tomatoes into chunks, discarding the seeds. Mix together the chicken, tomatoes, spring onions and yoghurt. Add pesto if you wish.
Serve the jacket potatoes with a portion of the chicken mixture.

title: Chicken Jalfrezi
steps: Heat the oil in a non-stick pan on a high heat. Add the chicken pieces and cook for 2 minutes. Add the cumin, turmeric and chilli powder. Mix well and fry on a medium heat for 3 to 4 mins.
Remove the chicken and set aside. Fry the sliced onions on a medium heat for 7 minutes, until soft and golden. Blend the tinned tomatoes.
Add the ginger, garlic and peppers to the pan and cook for 2 minutes, then add the tomatoes and cook for another 2 minutes. Stir in the coriander powder, garam masala and cumin seeds.
Add the chicken pieces back to the pan, stir well and cook for a couple of minutes. Add the green chillies and pour in the water. Cover and simmer on low heat for 10 to 15 minutes.
Once the chicken is tender and the sauce has thickened, turn off the heat and scatter over the chopped coriander.

title: Chicken Pizza Naan
ingredients: 150g skinless, boneless roast chicken breast, sliced
Quarter of a cucumber, finely chopped
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan, gas mark 6).
Put the spring onions, tomatoes, tomato purée and mixed herbs into a saucepan over a medium heat. Cook for 5 to 8 minutes, until reduced and thickened.
Arrange the naan on a baking sheet, divide the tomato mixture between them. Add the chicken, then sprinkle over the cheese. Bake for 5 to 6 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix together the salad ingredients, and serve with the naan once ready.

title: Chilli Con Carne
steps: Heat a large saucepan and add the minced beef, a handful at a time, cooking until browned.
Add the onion and garlic, then cook for another 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the chopped tomatoes, tomato purée, spices, red pepper, mushrooms, kidney beans and stock. Stir well, bring to the boil, then simmer gently for 15 to 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to pack instructions.
Season the chilli with pepper and serve with the boiled rice.

title: Chicken and Sweetcorn Soup
ingredients: 250g potatoes, scrubbed and chopped into small chunks
2 tablespoons cornflour, mixed with 2 tablespoons cold water
steps: Melt the spread in a large saucepan and gently cook the onion for 2 to 3 minutes, until starting to soften.
Add the potatoes and stock. Bring to the boil, then simmer, part-covered, for about 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Add the sweetcorn (drained if tinned), milk and chicken, and cook gently for 3 or 4 minutes.
Add the cornflour mixture and stir until thickened. Add the parsley and cook for another few moments. Ladle into bowls and serve.

title: Classic Cottage Pie
steps: Cook the potatoes in a large saucepan of boiling water for about 20 minutes until tender.
While the potatoes are cooking, heat a large saucepan. Add the minced beef a handful at a time, cooking until browned.
Stir in the onion, carrot, courgette, green beans and mixed herbs, then add 450ml water. Simmer without a lid for about 20 minutes.
Turn the grill on to preheat and warm a large baking dish. Drain and mash the potatoes, seasoning with pepper.
Add the sweetcorn to the mince, sprinkle in the gravy granules and stir until thickened. Tip into the baking dish. Spoon the mash on top and pop under the grill.
Once the top has browned, remove from the grill and leave to cool for a couple of minutes before serving.

title: Easy Vegetable Soup
ingredients: 50g sugar snap peas or green beans, sliced
steps: Heat the oil in a large saucepan. Add the onion, carrots, leek and celery, and fry gently for 3 or 4 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, tomato purée, beans, frozen peas and stock. Bring to the boil and add the pasta, herbs and pepper.
Lower the heat and simmer for 15 minutes or until the pasta is cooked, stirring frequently.
Turn off the heat and leave to cool for a few minutes. Ladle into bowls and serve with a slice of wholemeal bread.

title: Couscous with Chicken and Peas
steps: Put the couscous in a large bowl and cover with boiling water. Leave for 10 to 15 minutes to swell and cool, then stir with a fork to fluff up the grains.
Add the chicken and tomatoes, squeeze over the lemon juice, stir to combine.

title: Banana and Raspberry Porridge
steps: Put the porridge oats into a saucepan with the milk and water. Heat, stirring constantly, until the porridge thickens. Reduce the heat and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes.
Meanwhile, mash the bananas in a bowl. Stir half into the porridge. Add the raspberries to the rest of the banana and squash them a little.
Share the porridge between 6 warm bowls and top each portion with the yoghurt and the raspberry mixture.

title: Crispy Potato and Courgette Fritters with Salsa
ingredients: 1 teaspoon red or white wine vinegar
steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan, gas mark 7) and find a medium-sized baking sheet.
Put the grated potato and courgette onto kitchen paper and press out as much water as possible. Add to a bowl with the onion and egg, and mix well.
Take a large spoonful of the mixture, shape into a rough ball, then put on the baking sheet. Repeat to make 12 fritters. Bake for about 20 minutes, until crisp.
Meanwhile, make the salsa by mixing the tomatoes, sweetcorn, red onion, oil and vinegar.
Once the fritters are cooked, serve immediately with the salsa and a simple green salad.

title: Homemade Fish Fingers with Sweet Potato Wedges
ingredients: 4 medium-sized sweet potatoes, scrubbed, halved and each half cut into 4 wedges
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan, gas mark 6). Put the wedges into a roasting tin, add 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, toss to coat, and bake for 45 minutes.
Grease a baking tray. Beat the egg in a shallow dish. Sprinkle the breadcrumbs onto a plate.
Cut the fish into fingers, season with pepper. Dip in egg, roll in breadcrumbs and arrange on the baking tray.
When the potatoes have been in the oven for 25 minutes, turn them over. At the same time, put the fish fingers in the oven. Cook for 20 minutes, until crisp and golden.
Cook the vegetables in boiling water 5 minutes before ready. Drain and serve everything together.

title: Easy Overnight Oats
steps: In a large bowl, mix together the porridge oats, cranberries, apricots and sultanas. Add the apple juice and stir well. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, share the soaked mixture between 4 cereal bowls.
Spoon 2 tablespoons of natural yoghurt onto each portion, and share the berries between the bowls.

title: Easy Carrot Soup
ingredients: Small bunch of fresh coriander, chopped (optional)
steps: Put the onion and carrots into a large saucepan with the stock, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer, partially covered, for 20 to 25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
Blend the soup to a purée using a handheld stick blender. Add most of the soft cheese and blend again.
Reheat gently, then serve garnished with the remaining cheese, a little black pepper and coriander. Enjoy with wholemeal bread.

title: Easy Frozen Banana Ice Cream
steps: Peel and slice the bananas – use 1 banana per person.
Freeze for at least 2 hours, or overnight.
Add to a food processor and blend until smooth and creamy. Scoop into bowls and serve!

title: English Breakfast Muffins
steps: Preheat the grill. Toast the muffins on the cut sides only.
Meanwhile, beat the eggs and milk together. Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick saucepan and add the eggs. Cook and stir to scramble them.

Spread half the toasted muffins with reduced-fat soft cheese and top with a slice of ham. Share the scrambled egg between the muffins, season with black pepper, then top with the remaining muffin halves.

title: Eggs Paprika
ingredients: 2 medium onions, sliced into thin rings
3 teaspoons mild paprika, plus extra for sprinkling
steps: Cook the eggs in gently boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes to hard-boil them.
While the eggs are cooking, heat the vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan. Add the onions and cook over a medium-low heat for about 10 minutes until very soft.
Add the tomatoes and paprika to the onions, and cook for another 5 to 8 minutes.
Shell the eggs and cut each into 4. Toast the bread, then top each slice with a quarter of the tomato mixture and 4 egg quarters. Sprinkle with extra paprika.

title: Eyeball Halloween Meatballs
ingredients: Half a stock cube (reduced-salt) with 150ml boiled water
steps: Mix together the minced turkey with half the chopped onion, all the carrot and mixed herbs.
Form the mixture into 8 meatballs, using wet hands to prevent sticking.
Heat the oil in a large non-stick frying pan, then add the meatballs. Fry gently, turning occasionally, until lightly browned.
Add the remaining onion, tomatoes and the stock. Season with black pepper. Heat until boiling, then reduce the heat and cook for 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the tagliatelle for 10 to 12 minutes in gently boiling water.
Share the tagliatelle between 4 serving plates. Serve with the meatballs and sauce, topping each meatball with a mozzarella ball and cherry tomato half.

title: Falafels
ingredients: 400g tinned chickpeas in water, rinsed and drained
A quarter of a cucumber, sliced
steps: Put the onion and garlic in a food processor and chop finely.
Add the chickpeas, breadcrumbs, cumin, coriander, parsley and olive oil, and mix.
Shape into 16 balls and place on a greased or lined baking tray. Chill for 30 minutes.
Place tray into a preheated 200°C oven (180°C fan, gas mark 6) and bake for about 25 minutes, turning the falafel to prevent sticking.
To assemble, fill each pitta with lettuce, sliced tomatoes and cucumber, falafel and some yoghurt.

title: Fast Chicken Noodle Soup
ingredients: 250g boneless, skinless roast chicken breasts, sliced
steps: Put the noodles into a heatproof bowl and cover them with boiling water. Leave to soak for 6 minutes.
Meanwhile, pour the stock into a large saucepan and bring to the boil. Add the vegetables and simmer for 5 minutes.
Drain the noodles, then add them to the saucepan with the sliced chicken.
Heat for 1 to 2 minutes, then ladle into bowls.
Serve each portion sprinkled with a few drops of soy sauce.

title: Finger Licking Chicken Pittas
ingredients: 150g skinless uncooked chicken (thigh or breast), cut into strips
steps: Mix together the yoghurt, tomato purée and curry paste. Add the chicken and toss to coat. Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Preheat a non-stick frying pan over a medium heat. Add the teaspoon of vegetable oil.
Put the marinated chicken pieces into the heated pan and stir-fry over a medium heat until cooked but still juicy (about 5 to 8 minutes).
Warm and split open the pitta breads and fill them with shredded lettuce. Add the cooked chicken, then serve with cherry tomatoes.

title: Four Seasons Pizza
ingredients: 25g pineapple pieces in natural juice, drained
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan 180°C, gas mark 6). Place the pizza base on a large baking sheet.
Spoon the tomato purée on top and spread evenly. Arrange the tomatoes on top.
Scatter with half the mozzarella, then sprinkle with the herbs.
Arrange the ham over a quarter of the pizza with the pineapple. Arrange the mushrooms over a second quarter. Put the cooked chicken over a third quarter. Leave the last quarter plain.
Sprinkle the rest of the mozzarella over the whole pizza, then bake for 12 to 15 minutes until the cheese is bubbling.

title: Greek Yoghurt and Raspberry Chill
steps: Preheat the grill. Sprinkle the porridge oats onto a baking sheet and grill until light brown, about 2 or 3 minutes.
Set the baking sheet aside and let the oats cool.
Put half the raspberries into a mixing bowl and squash them down slightly with a fork.
Divide the whole raspberries between 4 serving glasses, saving 4 for decoration.
Add the yoghurt and cooled oats to the bowl of crushed raspberries and stir gently. Spoon into the serving glasses and top each with a whole raspberry. Chill until ready to serve.

title: Homemade Fish and Chips
ingredients: 4 potatoes, scrubbed, each cut into 8 wedges
1 egg, beaten with 2 tbsp cold water
4 fillets skinless white fish (haddock, cod or pollock)
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (fan 180°C, gas mark 6). Lightly grease a baking sheet with vegetable oil.
Put the potato wedges into a roasting tin. Add the remaining vegetable oil and toss to coat.
Transfer to the oven to bake for 35 to 40 minutes, turning them over after 20 minutes.

Sprinkle the breadcrumbs onto a large plate. Dip each fish fillet in the beaten egg, then coat in the breadcrumbs. Place on the baking sheet, then transfer to the oven when you turn the potatoes, for 15 to 20 minutes.
Heat the mushy peas in a saucepan, then serve with the fish and chips.

title: Greek Salad
ingredients: Half a cucumber, sliced and quartered
steps: Mix the lemon juice, olive oil, oregano and black pepper together.
Combine the salad vegetables, olives and dressing, mix well and put in a serving bowl.
Crumble the cheese over the top.

title: Healthier Full English Breakfast
ingredients: 4 lean back bacon rashers, fat trimmed
250g closed-cup mushrooms, washed and thickly sliced
420g reduced-salt and sugar baked beans in tomato sauce
steps: Preheat the grill.
Arrange the bacon and halved tomatoes on the rack and cook for 5 to 6 minutes, turning the bacon once.
Meanwhile, put the mushrooms into a large frying pan with the water. Heat and simmer for 5 minutes.
At the same time, heat the baked beans in a separate saucepan.
Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan and crack in the eggs. Cook over a medium heat for about 2 minutes, then transfer to the grill to set the surface.
Share the bacon and tomatoes between 4 warm serving plates. Share out the baked beans and mushrooms, then place 1 egg on each plate.

title: Vegetable and Lentil Soup
steps: Put the carrots, onion, red pepper, lentils and ginger into a large saucepan. Add the stock and bring to the boil.
Once boiling, reduce the heat and simmer, partially covered, for 25 minutes, until the vegetables and lentils are tender.
Blend the soup using a hand-held stick blender. Season to taste with pepper.
Ladle the soup into 4 bowls and swirl the yoghurt on top. Serve with wholemeal rolls if you like.

title: Jerk-Style Chicken Skewers
ingredients: 4 wooden skewers, soaked in water for 30 minutes
1 to 2 teaspoons jerk seasoning, to taste
2 skinless, boneless chicken breasts, cut into 12 large chunks
steps: Mix the garlic, lemon juice and olive oil with 1 teaspoon of jerk seasoning. Stir in the chicken and set aside while you prepare the vegetables.
Thread a piece of pepper onto a skewer, then onion, then chicken, finishing with onion.
Cook on the barbecue for 8 to 10 minutes, turning frequently. Alternatively, cook under a preheated grill.
Meanwhile cook the sweetcorn cobettes in boiling water, or on the barbecue for 6 to 8 minutes.

title: Jollof Rice
steps: Liquidise the tomatoes, onion and pepper in a blender. Tip into a large saucepan, cover and cook over medium-high heat for 6 to 8 minutes.
Add the tomato purée, bay leaf, garlic, curry powder and thyme. Simmer for a further couple of minutes, then add the stock and bring to boil.
Once boiling, add the rice and stir well, then turn down the heat and cover with a lid.
Allow to simmer over a low heat for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Check and stir occasionally, adding a little extra water if needed.
Once the rice is cooked, turn off the heat and leave to cool for a minute or two before serving.

title: Macaroni Cheese with Tomatoes
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan 170°C, gas mark 5).
Cook the macaroni in a large saucepan of boiling water for 8 to 10 minutes until tender. Drain well and return to the saucepan.
Melt the low-fat spread in a large saucepan and cook the onion for 3 or 4 minutes until softened.
Remove from the heat and stir in the flour. Return to the heat and cook gently for about 1 minute. Remove from the heat again and add in the milk a little at a time, stirring well.
Return to the heat and stir the sauce constantly until thick and smooth. Add two-thirds of the cheese and the mustard. Season with pepper.
Add the hot cheese sauce to the pan with the cooked pasta and mix well. Tip into a baking dish, top with the tomato slices and sprinkle the remaining cheese on top.
Bake for 15 to 20 minutes until piping hot and the cheese on top is starting to brown.

title: Mediterranean Potato Bake
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (180°C fan, gas mark 6).
Put the potatoes, chopped vegetables and pine nuts in a large roasting tin.
Drizzle with the oil and toss to coat. Bake for 20 minutes, or until tender.
Add the pesto, if using, and bake for another 5 minutes. Serve immediately.

title: Meatballs and Sauce
ingredients: 200g any type of mince (lean if possible)
200g any type of dried pasta, like fusilli
steps: Mix some of the chopped onion and garlic with the mince. Shape the mince into small balls about half the size of a golf ball.
Heat the oil in a non-stick frying pan and brown the meatballs on all sides. Remove and put onto a plate.
Add the remaining onion to the frying pan and cook for 2 to 3 minutes until soft. Add the remaining garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the tomatoes, tomato purée, herbs, mushrooms and peppers to the pan with 150ml water. Bring to the boil, then add the meatballs. Reduce the heat, cover and simmer for 30 minutes.
About 10 minutes before serving, put the pasta on to cook. Serve with the meatballs and tomato sauce.

title: Mexican Bean and Tomato Soup
ingredients: Half a teaspoon chilli powder or chilli flakes (optional)
1 tin or 1 medium-sized cob of sweetcorn
1 tablespoon parsley or fresh coriander, chopped (optional)
steps: Heat the oil in a large saucepan and gently cook the garlic and onion for about 3 minutes, until softened.
Add the chilli and the tomatoes, and cook for a couple of minutes.
Pour in the stock and add the tomato purée, mixed beans and sweetcorn. Stir everything to mix well.
Bring to the boil then turn the heat down and simmer, uncovered, for 15 to 20 minutes.
Add the coriander or parsley (if using) and season with some pepper. Ladle into warm bowls and serve with a wholemeal roll.

title: Mushroom Risotto
ingredients: 5 spring onions, washed, trimmed and chopped
3 handfuls of cup or button mushrooms, washed and sliced
steps: Heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan or saucepan, and cook the spring onions over a medium-high heat for a few seconds.
Turn the heat down, then add the rice and fry over a low heat for about a minute, stirring all the time, until it looks glossy.
Add the garlic and mushrooms, and cook for a minute, until the garlic is fragrant.
Pour in about half the hot stock and stir well. Cook over a medium heat for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring often and slowly adding the remaining stock a little at a time.
Add the peas, stirring gently. Cook for another couple of minutes to allow them to warm through. Check the seasoning and serve.

title: Sausage and Mash
ingredients: 300g swede or turnip, cut into chunks
2 tablespoons cornflour blended with 1 tablespoon of cold water
steps: Cook the carrots, swede or turnip and potatoes in a large saucepan of gently boiling water for about 20 minutes, until tender.
Meanwhile, preheat the grill. Arrange the sausages on the grill rack and grill for 10 to 12 minutes, turning often.
At the same time, heat the vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan and add the onion, cooking until soft and lightly browned.
Pour in the stock and water, add the herbs, then simmer for 4 to 5 minutes. Add the blended cornflour and stir until thickened. Keep hot over a low heat.
Drain and mash the vegetables, seasoning with black pepper. Serve 2 sausages per person with the red onion gravy.

title: Pasta Ratatouille Bake
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan, gas mark 4).
Cook the macaroni according to pack instructions, then drain. Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large saucepan, then cook the onion and garlic slowly until tender and golden.
Stir in the herbs, tomatoes, courgettes, beans and stock. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Combine the pasta and vegetables, and season with black pepper. Transfer to a baking dish and sprinkle the cheese on top.
Bake for 30 to 35 minutes.

title: Spicy Turkey Stuffed Peppers
ingredients: 4 peppers, any colour, deseeded and cut in half
300g skinless turkey breasts, cut into chunks
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan 170°C, gas mark 5). Arrange the peppers in a roasting pan, cut side up.
Bring a large saucepan of water to a simmer, add the rice and cook for 12 to 15 minutes until tender.

While the rice is cooking, heat the vegetable oil in a large frying pan and stir-fry the onion and garlic for about 3 minutes. Add the turkey and stir-fry for about 5 minutes. Add the tomatoes, peas, paprika and herbs, then remove from the heat.
Drain the rice, stir it into the tomato mixture and season. Spoon the filling into the pepper halves.
Cover with foil, bake for 20 to 25 minutes and then serve.

title: Pesto and Parmesan Salmon
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (180°C fan, gas mark 6). Add all the vegetables (except the potatoes) to a roasting tray. Drizzle over the oil, season with black pepper and mix well. Cook in the oven for 30 minutes.

While the vegetables are roasting, line a baking tray with non-stick baking parchment, and arrange the salmon fillets on it. In a bowl, combine the breadcrumbs, pesto, cheese and lower-fat spread. Spoon the mixture evenly over the salmon pieces.
Once the vegetables have been cooking for 15 minutes, stir to prevent sticking. Return to the oven, along with the salmon, and bake for another 15 to 20 minutes.
When the fish goes in the oven, cook the potatoes in boiling water for 15 minutes or until soft. Serve with the salmon and oven-roasted mixed vegetables.

title: Pork and Bean Casserole
ingredients: 350g lean pork shoulder, cut into chunks
1 unpeeled medium apple, cored and chopped
2 tins haricot or cannellini beans in water, rinsed and drained
steps: Preheat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan, gas mark 4). Heat the vegetable oil in a large flameproof casserole dish. Add the chunks of pork and cook over a high heat for 3 to 4 minutes, until seared and browned.
Add the onion, garlic, carrots and apple. Cook, stirring, for 2 more minutes.
Add the tomatoes, beans, tomato purée, Worcestershire sauce, mixed herbs and stock. Season with some pepper and stir well.
Put the lid on and transfer to the oven to cook for 90 minutes. Serve, sprinkled with the chopped parsley.

title: Quick Chicken Couscous
ingredients: 250g skinless, boneless roast chicken breasts, chopped
steps: Put the couscous into a large mixing bowl and cover with the hot stock. Leave for 10 to 15 minutes to swell and cool, then stir to fluff up the grains.
Add the chicken, sweetcorn, tomatoes, cucumber, lemon juice and black pepper. Stir well to mix and divide between 4 bowls.

title: Roast Chicken Breast with Peppers
ingredients: 1 red pepper, deseeded and cut into strips
1 yellow pepper, deseeded and cut into strips
2 teaspoons dried oregano or mixed herbs
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (fan 170°C, gas mark 5). Put the potatoes onto the top shelf of the oven to bake.
Heat the vegetable oil in a flameproof casserole dish and stir-fry the onion and peppers over a high heat until softened, about 3 minutes.

Put the paprika, herbs and flour into a shallow dish and season with pepper. Dip each chicken breast into the mixture, coating them on both sides. Transfer to the casserole dish, on top of the onions and peppers.
Pour in the stock, then cover the dish. Put in the oven when the potatoes have been baking for 30 minutes. Bake for 35 minutes.
Around 10 minutes before the chicken and potatoes are ready, cook the broccoli in boiling water for 8 to 10 minutes. Drain and serve with the rest of the meal.

title: Salmon with Spring Onion Mash
ingredients: 1kg potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
steps: Cook the potatoes in boiling water for 20 minutes, adding the spring onions to the saucepan 5 minutes before the end of cooking time.

When the potatoes have been cooking for 10 minutes, heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan. Add the salmon, skin side down. Cook over a high heat for 2 to 3 minutes, then turn and cook for a further 3 to 4 minutes.
At this point, put the broccoli on to cook in boiling water for 5 to 6 minutes.
Drain the potatoes and spring onions and mash thoroughly. Beat in the milk and parsley. Reheat on the hob for 1 minute.
Share the mash between 4 warmed plates and arrange the salmon fillets on top. Serve with the broccoli.

title: Shakshuka
ingredients: Half a red bell pepper, deseeded and finely chopped
Half a yellow bell pepper, deseeded and finely chopped
steps: Heat the oil in a large non-stick frying pan on medium heat. Add the onion, peppers and garlic, and cook for about 5 minutes, until the vegetables soften.

Stir in the aubergine and cook for another 5 minutes until golden-brown. Add the tomatoes, tomato purée, cumin and cayenne, and stir well. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and cook for about 10 minutes.
Stir in the chickpeas, cover and cook for further 5 to 8 minutes. Once the chickpeas have softened, stir in the chopped spinach and lower the heat.
Using a wooden spoon, create 6 wells in the mixture and crack 1 egg into each. Cover the pan and cook for about 6 to 8 minutes on low, until the egg whites set.
Take the mixture off the heat and leave to cool for a few minutes. Scatter with chopped coriander and serve.

title: Apple, Apricot and Sultana Squares
ingredients: 1 apple, cored and chopped into small chunks
half a teaspoon ground mixed spice (optional)
steps: Preheat oven to 180C (fan 160C, gas mark 4). Grease a 23cm square cake tin with reduced-fat spread, then line base with baking parchment.
Melt spread in large saucepan with golden syrup. Take care mixture doesn't get too hot.
Remove pan from heat and add porridge oats, apple, apricots, sultanas and mixed spice. Stir well.
Add beaten egg and mix well again.
Tip mixture into prepared tin and level surface. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until firm.
Cool in tin for about 20 minutes, then cut into 16 squares.

title: Asaro Potato Porridge
ingredients: 2 red bell peppers, deseeded and chopped
425g sweet potato, peeled and cut into cubes
425g white potato, peeled and cut into cubes
steps: Add scotch bonnet, onion and red pepper to food blender with 300ml water, blend to smooth paste.
Pour blended ingredients into large pot, add white potato, sweet potato, palm oil, stock cube and crayfish. Top with water until potatoes covered by 3cm.
Cover pot and boil on medium-low heat for 20 minutes until potatoes soft enough to mash.
Lower heat and gently crush potatoes with wooden spoon, combining with sauce.
Stir in kale and simmer 3-5 minutes.
Create 4 wells in mixture, add hard-boiled eggs, serve hot.

title: Bajan Cou Cou with Spicy Fish
ingredients: 650g white fish fillets (such as coley, cod or pollock), cleaned and washed
half a yellow pepper, deseeded and cut into strips
half a red pepper, deseeded and cut into strips
1 small scotch bonnet pepper, finely chopped (optional)
steps: Add the parsley, celery, spring onion, onion, ginger, thyme, red chilli and lime juice to a food processor and pulse to a coarse green marinade. Put the fish in a bowl, pour in the mixture, cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.

Heat the oil in a large pan and add the onions and peppers. Cook for 5 minutes until softened, then add thyme and scotch bonnet (if using). Add the marinated fish, tomatoes and salt, cover and simmer on low for 10 to 15 minutes.
Mix cornmeal with enough cold water to form a smooth paste. Set aside.
Simmer okra in 400ml water for 5 minutes. Drain okra, reserving the okra water.
Gradually whisk cornmeal paste into the hot okra water in the pot over low heat, adding oil and remaining water and whisking until smooth.
Switch to a wooden spoon and stir for 2 to 3 minutes until thickened. Add cooked okra and mix through. Remove from heat.
Scatter parsley over the fish and serve with the cou cou.

title: Baked Tomatoes on Toast
ingredients: 2 tablespoons white or wholemeal dried breadcrumbs
4 teaspoons reduced-fat hard cheese, finely grated
steps: Preheat the oven to 190C (fan oven 170C, gas mark 5).
Arrange the tomatoes, cut sides up, on a baking sheet. Sprinkle with the dried herbs and chives (if using) and season with black pepper. Bake for 10 minutes.
Sprinkle the breadcrumbs and cheese over the tomatoes, and bake for another 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, toast the bread. Place the toast on warmed plates and share the tomatoes between them.

title: Banana and Apricot Bagels
steps: Preheat the grill. Lightly toast the cut sides of each bagel.
Mix the low-fat soft cheese and apricots together and spread over the bagel bases. Top with sliced banana then sandwich the bagel tops in place.

title: Banana and Sultana Bread and Butter Pudding
steps: Preheat the oven to 180C (fan oven 160C, gas mark 4).
Grease a 1.2 litre baking dish with a little reduced fat spread. Spread the rest over the bread, then cut each slice into 4 triangles.
Arrange the bread slices in the dish with the sliced bananas and sultanas or raisins.
Beat together the eggs, milk and vanilla extract. Stir in the sugar and allow a few minutes for it to dissolve. Pour the mixture over the bread, and let it soak in for 5 minutes.
Bake on the middle shelf of the oven for about 25 to 30 minutes, until set and golden brown.

title: Bang-Tasty Roast Chicken Drumsticks
ingredients: 880g potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
steps: Put the tomato purée, reduced-salt soy sauce and lemon juice into a mixing bowl. Mix well and season with black pepper. Add the chicken drumsticks, turning to coat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 200C/fan oven 180C/gas mark 6. Arrange the drumsticks in a foil-lined roasting tin and roast for 30 minutes, brushing with the remaining glaze after 20 minutes.
Boil the potatoes and carrots separately until tender, about 20 minutes. Cook peas 5 minutes before the chicken is ready.
Drain and mash the potatoes, beat in the milk and season with black pepper. Serve with the chicken drumsticks, carrots and peas.

title: Bariis Iskukaris
ingredients: 4 or 5 cloves of garlic, finely chopped
1 cinnamon stick, or 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
600g skinless chicken thighs with bone, washed
steps: Heat the oil in a large saucepan and gently fry the onions and garlic on medium heat for 2 minutes. Add the cinnamon, cumin seeds and chopped tomatoes, and cook for 3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the chicken, turmeric, cumin powder, coriander, crumbled seasoning cube and grated carrot. Mix well and cook on medium-high heat for about 5 minutes, stirring regularly.
Add 400ml water, cover the pot and leave to cook on medium heat for 15 minutes until the chicken is nearly done.
Add the rice and stir until well coated. Add 200ml cold water, stir, cover and cook on medium-high until bubbling, then reduce to low and steam until rice is tender, about 10 to 15 minutes.
Serve topped with fresh coriander and accompanied by a green salad.

title: Roast Beef Sandwiches
ingredients: half a small red onion, thinly sliced
4 slices lean roast beef, fat trimmed off
steps: In a bowl, mix together the carrot, beetroot and red onion with the vinegar. Season with black pepper.

Spread each slice of bread with 1 teaspoon of mayonnaise, then top 4 of them with the lettuce leaves and the roast beef. Share the salad between them, then sandwich together with the remaining slices of bread.
Cut in half and serve at once, or wrap and keep cool to serve later.

title: Bengali Chicken Curry
ingredients: 500g skinless chicken pieces, on the bone
1 teaspoon red chilli powder, or to taste
2 green bell peppers, deseeded and cut into chunks
300g basmati rice, cooked according to packet instructions
steps: Mix the chicken with the marinade ingredients in a large bowl and set aside.
Heat the oil in a large non-stick pan. Add the cloves, cardamom, cinnamon sticks and bay leaves and allow to sizzle for a minute.
Add the onions and cumin seeds, and stir-fry over a high heat for about 10 minutes.
Mix in the chilli powder, turmeric, paprika and chicken. Cook on high heat for about 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the hot water, then lower the heat to medium, cover and cook for 20 minutes.
Add the green peppers, cover and cook for a further 5 to 10 minutes until peppers are tender and chicken is cooked through. Serve with rice.

title: Bharelu Shaak
ingredients: 4 long Turkish peppers, slit and de-seeded
steps: Pre-heat the oven to 200C (180C fan, gas mark 5). In a deep baking tray, add a small drop of oil and 240ml water. Make slits in all the vegetables, deep enough for stuffing.
In a separate bowl, combine all the stuffing ingredients and mix well. Stuff a few spoonfuls into each vegetable.
Place the stuffed vegetables into the oiled tray. Sprinkle any leftover stuffing on top, cover with foil, and bake for 40 minutes or until the potatoes are soft.
Remove from the oven and cool for a few minutes before serving.

title: Blueberry and Banana Muffins
ingredients: 125g lower-fat spread, melted and cooled slightly
50g soft brown or light muscovado sugar
steps: Preheat the oven to 180C (fan 160C, gas mark 4). Line a muffin tin with 8 paper muffin cases.
Mix together the cooled lower-fat spread, milk, vanilla extract, mashed banana, apple purée and eggs in a bowl.
In a separate large mixing bowl, combine the flour, mixed spice, sugar, blueberries and porridge oats.
Slowly tip the wet ingredients into the dry ones, gently mixing until combined.
Divide the mixture evenly between the muffin cases, then bake for 25 to 30 minutes until firm and golden. Leave to cool on a wire rack.

title: Blueberry and Banana Smoothie
steps: Tip the blueberries into a blender and slice in the bananas.
Add the milk and vanilla extract. Whizz together for a few seconds until smooth, then pour into 4 glasses and serve at once.

title: Healthy Beans on Toast
ingredients: 1 red pepper, deseeded and finely chopped
1 tin (420g) reduced-salt and reduced-sugar baked beans
steps: Put the pepper and spring onions into a large saucepan with 3 tablespoons of water. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes over a low heat until the water has evaporated.
Add the beans, mushrooms and cherry tomatoes. Heat gently for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring often, until the beans are piping hot.
Meanwhile, toast the bread, then spread each piece with the lower-fat spread. Pile the beans on to the toast and serve, sprinkled with black pepper.

title: Caribbean Chicken Salad
ingredients: 150g pineapple pieces in natural juice, drained
steps: Cook the rice in a saucepan of gently boiling water for about 15 minutes until tender. Drain and rinse under cold water to cool quickly, then drain again.
Mix together the rice, pineapple, bananas, grapes, sultanas and lemon juice.
In a large bowl, mix together the yoghurt and mayonnaise. Add the rice mixture and chicken. Season with pepper and mix well.
Arrange the lettuce on 4 serving plates and share the chicken mixture between them. Serve at once.

title: Caribbean Tofu and Sweet Potato Curry with Rice and Peas
ingredients: 125g dried kidney beans, soaked overnight and rinsed
125g white rice (long grain or basmati), washed and drained
300g extra-firm tofu, patted dry and cut into 2.5cm cubes
half a scotch bonnet pepper, deseeded and chopped (optional)
125g sweet potatoes, peeled and chopped into bite-sized chunks
steps: Put the beans in a large pot. Add the onions, fresh thyme, spring onions, garlic, black pepper, all-purpose seasoning, scotch bonnet and coconut milk. Cover with 400ml of water and boil on medium-low heat for 40 to 50 minutes until beans are soft.
Add the washed rice to the beans and mix. Ensure there is about 2.5cm of water above the rice and beans, cover and cook on low heat for 30 minutes until the rice is tender.
Heat 2 teaspoons of oil in a large non-stick pan. Add the tofu and fry on each side until golden. Remove and drain on paper towel.

Heat the remaining oil in the same pan. Add onions, garlic and pepper and cook for 2 minutes. Add turmeric, paprika, curry powder, scotch bonnet, dried thyme, seasoning cube and bay leaf. Stir and cook for 2 minutes. Add sweet potato and mix thoroughly.
Add about 500ml of water, bring to the boil, cover and simmer for 10 to 12 minutes until sweet potatoes are soft.
Add spring onion and tofu to the curry, bring back to the boil and cook for 5 minutes. Fluff the rice with a fork and serve with a portion of curry, scattered with fresh coriander.

title: Carrot and Courgette Muffins
ingredients: 125g lower-fat spread, melted and cooled slightly
steps: Preheat the oven to 180C (fan oven 160C, gas mark 4). Line a muffin tin with 8 paper muffin cases.
Mix together the cooled lower-fat spread, milk and eggs. Stir in the grated carrot and courgette.
In a separate large mixing bowl, combine the flour, baking powder, dried herbs and porridge oats.
Stir the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients, taking care not to over-mix. Spoon into the muffin cases and bake for 25 to 30 minutes until firm and golden. Cool on a wire rack.

title: Carrot and Sultana Pancakes
steps: Put the flour, mixed spice, egg, grated carrot, yoghurt and milk into a large mixing bowl and whisk together thoroughly.
Stir in the sultanas.
Heat a non-stick frying pan over medium heat and add a few drops of oil. Cook tablespoonfuls of the batter in batches, turning after 1 to 2 minutes to cook the other side.
Make 8 mini pancakes altogether, adding a further 2 to 3 drops of oil to the frying pan with each batch.

title: Chargrilled Pineapple
ingredients: 1 pineapple (or 1 tin of pineapple slices, in juice)
steps: Cut off the top and bottom of the pineapple, then remove the peel. Cut into 4 thick slices and remove the core of each with a small knife.
Barbecue for 2 to 3 minutes on each side.
Serve each slice sprinkled with coconut and with a wedge of lime on the side. Add a spoonful of yoghurt if you like.

title: Cheesy Coleslaw with Wholemeal Pitta
ingredients: 1 thin slice of white cabbage, shredded
2 spring onions or a slice of onion, chopped
20g reduced-fat cheddar cheese, finely chopped or grated
steps: In a bowl, mix the mayonnaise and yoghurt.
Add the vegetables, cheese and parsley (if using). Mix well.
Carefully split open the pitta bread and fill with the coleslaw.

title: Cheesy Ham and Leek Bake
steps: Simmer leeks in water for 10-12 minutes until tender, drain and cool slightly.
Wrap ham around each leek and arrange in a baking dish. Preheat oven to 200C (180C fan, gas mark 6).
Heat flour, spread and milk in a saucepan, whisking constantly until thick and smooth.
Add cheese and mustard, season with pepper, stir until melted and pour over leeks.
Bake 15-20 minutes until heated through and golden.

title: Cherry Berry Crumble
ingredients: 12 tablespoons low-fat, lower-sugar plain yoghurt, to serve
steps: Preheat oven to 180C, fan 160C, gas mark 4.
Place frozen cherries and berries in a baking dish without thawing.
Mix flour with lower-fat spread by rubbing with fingertips until a breadcrumb texture forms, then stir in oats and sugar.
Sprinkle crumble topping over fruit, place dish on a baking tray, bake 30-35 minutes.
Serve with 2 tablespoons of yoghurt per person.

title: Chicken and Banana Korma
steps: Heat oil in a large saucepan. Add chicken, onion, apple and garlic; cook 2-3 minutes stirring often. Add curry paste and cook briefly.
Add stock, raisins and apricots. Bring to boil, reduce heat, simmer partially covered for 35 minutes.
Cook brown rice in simmering water for 30-35 minutes.
Blend cornflour with 2 tablespoons cold water, add to curry stirring until thickened. Add banana slices and cook gently. Season with pepper.
Serve topped with 1 tablespoon yoghurt alongside rice, garnished with coriander.

title: Chicken Pasta with Peas
ingredients: 175g cooked chicken, chopped into small chunks
steps: Boil water in a large pan, add pasta, stir once and cook 10-12 minutes until al dente.
Add peas 2-3 minutes before pasta finishes cooking.
Drain pasta and peas, return to pan. Add chicken, horseradish, crème fraîche and parsley, season with pepper and stir well.
Reheat gently over low heat until warm, divide among 4 bowls and serve.

title: Chilli Beef and Bean Burgers
ingredients: 210g tinned red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
wholemeal breadcrumbs from 1 slice of bread
2 teaspoons mild chilli powder, or to taste
steps: Process onion in a food processor, then add beans, mince, breadcrumbs, chilli powder, egg and tomato purée; mix thoroughly.
Form mixture into 4 patties and refrigerate until ready to cook.
Barbecue or grill for 5-7 minutes per side.
Serve in rolls with sliced onion, tomato and lettuce leaves.

title: Chocolate Cherry Popcorn Cakes
steps: Preheat oven to 180C (160C fan, gas mark 4). Insert 14 paper bun cases into baking trays.
Heat oil in a heavy-based saucepan with a tight lid. Add corn, cover and shake to coat. Heat over medium-high, shaking occasionally as kernels pop.
Remove from heat when popping stops. Cool for 5 minutes.
Whisk egg whites with cocoa powder in a bowl. Stir in cooled popcorn and cherries. Divide among bun cases.
Bake 8-10 minutes until set. Cool completely before serving.

title: Coconut Rice Salad
ingredients: 1 bunch of spring onions, finely sliced
juice and grated zest of 1 lime
steps: Rinse rice, add to 250ml cold water, bring to boil, cover and cook for 25 minutes.
Cool rice in a sieve under cold running water.
Mix cooled rice with vegetables, lime juice and zest, and coriander.
Transfer to a serving dish, sprinkle with coconut, and refrigerate until serving.

title: Sweetcorn and Sweet Potato Fritters
ingredients: 350g sweetcorn (fresh, tinned and drained, or frozen and defrosted)
half a red chilli, de-seeded and sliced (optional)
1 handful of fresh coriander, roughly chopped (optional)
handful of fresh mint, finely chopped
steps: Mix flour and baking powder in a bowl. Add eggs and milk, whisk until a smooth batter forms. Stir in sweetcorn, sweet potato, spring onions, curry powder, and optional chilli and coriander.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Add tablespoons of batter and fry 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Remove cooked fritters onto a kitchen paper-lined plate. Work in batches until all batter is used.
Make the dip by mixing yoghurt, mint and pepper together. Serve alongside fritters.

title: Courgette, Rocket and Ham Pizza
ingredients: 2 thin and crispy pizza bases, 25cm wide
steps: Preheat oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6).
Spread 2 tablespoons tomato purée on each pizza base and sprinkle with herbs.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan, cook onion and courgette for 5-6 minutes until softened, then arrange on the pizzas.
Bake in oven for 10-12 minutes.
Remove from oven and top with rocket and torn ham, garnish with basil.

title: Creamy Hummus Dip with Pitta Bread and Vegetable Sticks
ingredients: 1 large wholemeal pitta bread, cut into strips
1 stick of celery, cut into sticks
steps: Put chickpeas, lemon juice, yoghurt, oil, spices and garlic in a bowl.
Using a hand blender, mix together until smooth.
Serve with pitta bread strips and carrot and celery sticks.

title: Crunchy Salad Pittas
ingredients: 2 tablespoons lemon juice or white wine vinegar
quarter of a small red cabbage, finely shredded
50g reduced-fat hard cheese, cut into tiny chunks
steps: Combine lemon juice or vinegar, olive oil and mustard in a bowl.
Toss with cabbage, carrot, onion, apple, sultanas, cheese and pepper until well mixed.
Warm pittas, split open, fill with salad mixture, and serve immediately or wrap for later.

title: Deresh Aloo Curry
ingredients: 1 tablespoon fresh ginger, peeled and grated
steps: Heat oil in a pan, add spices, bay leaves, onion, garlic and ginger. Cook on low heat for 3-4 minutes until browning.
Add potato, cover and cook for 5 minutes until softening begins.
Add okra, mix well, pour in water, stir and simmer uncovered for approximately 5 minutes until okra softens.

title: Egg Mayonnaise and Lettuce Bap
steps: Boil egg in water for 10 minutes, then cool in cold water.
Remove shell and mash egg with mayonnaise and pepper.
Fill bap with the egg mixture and top with shredded lettuce.

title: Ewa Oloyin with Plantain
ingredients: 300g honey beans or black-eyed beans, washed, soaked overnight and drained
2 medium-sized ripe plantains, peeled and cut into 4 or 5 pieces each
steps: Boil 700ml water in a large saucepan. Add beans and half the onion, cover and cook on medium-low heat for approximately 40 minutes. Add 200ml water if needed.
Add remaining onion, oil, ground pepper and salt to the pot. Stir well and cook for 5 minutes on low heat. Add chopped bell pepper, stir and simmer briefly.
About 10 minutes before beans are ready, steam plantain pieces until soft when pricked with a fork, approximately 12-15 minutes. Serve plantain alongside the beans.

title: Eyeball Juice Jellies
ingredients: 250ml unsweetened apple and raspberry fruit juice
steps: Peel lychees or split black grapes. Insert a green grape then a blueberry into each to form eyeballs.
Place one eyeball in each of 4 glasses and refrigerate remaining eyeballs.
Pour 90ml boiling water into a jug, sprinkle in gelatine, stir and leave to dissolve for 5 minutes.
Mix juice and cold water in a large jug. Add cooled gelatine and stir well.
Divide mixture among glasses, refrigerate for 2-3 hours until set.
Serve decorated with remaining eyeballs.

title: Fabulous Fish Pie
ingredients: 300g coley, pollock or other white fish fillets
steps: Boil potatoes for 15-20 minutes until tender, drain and mash with 2 tablespoons of the milk.

Preheat oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6). Combine remaining milk, spread and flour in a saucepan. Bring to boil over medium heat, stirring until thickened. Add parsley and peas, season with pepper.
Place fish chunks in an ovenproof dish. Pour sauce over the fish, top with mashed potato and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake 25-30 minutes until golden brown. Serve with steamed greens.

title: Fishy Toast Toppers
ingredients: 50g cooked prawns, peeled and thawed if frozen
half a cucumber, part finely chopped and part sliced to serve
small handful of watercress, to serve
steps: Mix mackerel, prawns, yoghurt, spring onions, tomato, chopped cucumber and lemon zest in a bowl. Season with black pepper and stir.
Toast the bread, spread the fish mixture on top. Serve with sliced cucumber and watercress.

title: Pancakes with Tomato and Mushrooms
steps: Combine flour, egg, yoghurt and milk in a bowl; whisk until a smooth batter forms.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan, add a quarter of the batter, tilt pan to spread, cook until bubbles appear and set, then flip and cook the other side. Repeat to make 4 pancakes.
In a separate pan, simmer mushrooms with stock for 5-6 minutes, then add tomatoes and herbs, cook for 2-3 minutes more.
Serve 1 pancake per person topped with the mushroom and tomato mixture.

title: Forest Fruit Milkshake
ingredients: 150g frozen summer berries or forest fruits
steps: Slice banana into a blender with the frozen fruit; no thawing needed.
Add yoghurt and milk, blend for 15-20 seconds.
Pour into 4 glasses and serve while cold.

title: Fruity French Toast
ingredients: 200g defrosted frozen summer berries or forest fruits
steps: Cut the slices of bread in half diagonally.
In a large shallow dish, beat together the egg, milk and vanilla extract. Add the pieces of fruit bread and leave for 2-3 minutes, turning once so each side is thoroughly soaked.
Heat a large non-stick frying pan and add the vegetable oil. Add the soaked bread and cook gently for 1-2 minutes until set and golden brown.
Turn over the pieces and cook on the other side for another 1-2 minutes.
Share the bread between 4 warm plates. Slice the bananas over the top and serve with the thawed berries.

title: Fruity Winter Salad
steps: Combine cabbage, red onion, carrot, apple, lemon juice and sultanas in a salad bowl.
Soften the soft cheese by beating it with a wooden spoon.
Mix the yoghurt into the softened cheese, pour over the salad, season with pepper and toss well to coat.

title: Haddock Florentine
steps: Preheat the grill. Fill the kettle and boil it. Grill the haddock fillets with the tomatoes at the side of the grill pan for 5 to 6 minutes until the fish is cooked.
Meanwhile, poach the eggs in gently simmering water for 4 to 5 minutes. Toast the slices of bread and spread them with reduced-fat spread.

Put the spinach in a large colander and slowly pour the boiling water over the leaves to wilt them. Drain, then share between 4 warm plates. Arrange the fish on top and place a poached egg onto each portion.
Sprinkle with black pepper and serve with the tomatoes and toast.

title: Ham and Fresh Coleslaw Pittas
steps: Mix the cabbage, carrot and spring onions together in a bowl. Add the yoghurt and stir to combine. Season with black pepper.
Warm the pittas under the grill or in a toaster. Split each pitta and stuff with a slice of ham and a generous spoonful of coleslaw.
Serve each pitta with cherry tomatoes and sliced cucumber on the side.

title: Harvest Vegetable Soup
steps: Combine the onion, leek, carrot, swede or turnip, potato and stock in a large saucepan. Bring to the boil, reduce the heat and simmer partially covered for 25 minutes until the vegetables are tender.

Mix the cornflour with 2 to 3 tablespoons of the milk into a smooth paste. Add the remaining milk and sweetcorn to the saucepan, then stir in the cornflour mixture. Heat gently while stirring until the soup thickens and begins to boil.
Season with pepper, ladle into bowls and serve with a wholemeal roll if desired.

title: Herby Pasta with Veg
ingredients: 100g sugar-snap peas or green beans, sliced
200g lower-fat soft cheese with garlic and herbs
steps: Cook the pasta in boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes until just tender.

Heat the oil in a large non-stick frying pan or wok. Add the spring onions, sugar-snap peas or green beans, broccoli and pepper, and stir-fry for 3 to 4 minutes until softened. Stir in the peas and tomatoes, then remove from the heat.
Drain the cooked pasta but keep 2 tablespoons of the cooking water. Add this water back to the saucepan with the cheese, milk and lemon zest. Heat, stirring, until the sauce is hot and smooth.
Tip the pasta and cooked vegetables into the sauce and stir gently for a minute to heat through. Divide among 4 bowls and serve.
ingredientNames: soft cheese with garlic and herbs

title: Hot Cheese and Salsa Turnovers
ingredients: quarter of a cucumber, finely chopped
1 tablespoon fresh coriander or parsley, chopped (optional)
steps: In a bowl, make the salsa by mixing together the tomatoes, red onion, cucumber and coriander or parsley (if using).
Heat a non-stick frying pan and add a few drops of vegetable oil. Add 1 of the tortillas and turn so its base is lightly oiled.
Sprinkle over a quarter of the salsa and a quarter of the cheese. Season with black pepper.
Cook over medium heat for 2 minutes until the cheese starts to melt and the tortilla is lightly browned. Fold in half, set aside and keep warm.
Cook the remaining tortillas the same way. Slice each in half and serve.

title: Hot Ham and Tomato Crumpets
steps: Preheat the grill and lightly toast the crumpets on both sides.
Spread 1 tablespoon of tomato purée on each crumpet, top with two slices of ham and arrange the sliced tomatoes on top.
Grill for 2 to 3 minutes until heated through, then serve immediately.

title: Hummus and Salad Wrap
steps: Spread the hummus over the wrap, then sprinkle on the carrot and lettuce.
Fold opposite edges of the wrap to keep the filling in, then roll up and cut in half.

title: Kedgeree with a Kick
ingredients: 200g skinless cod fillet (or pollock), cut into chunks
120g skinless smoked haddock fillet, cut into chunks
120g skinless salmon fillet, cut into chunks
steps: Cook the rice in boiling water until tender according to packet instructions. At the same time, hard-boil the eggs for 10 minutes.
Put the chunks of fish into a large frying pan and add a little water. Heat and simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until the fish is opaque. Drain.

Shell the eggs and quarter them. Drain the rice and add it to the fish with the curry powder, peas and parsley. Heat, stirring gently, for 2 to 3 minutes. Season with black pepper and serve topped with the eggs.

title: Kicking Prawn Salad
ingredients: 2 green chillies, deseeded and finely chopped
2 tablespoons fresh coriander or parsley, finely chopped
steps: Mix the olive oil, lime juice, chillies and herbs together in a large salad bowl to make the dressing.
Add the prawns and toss to coat. Refrigerate if preparing ahead.
Add the potatoes and cherry tomatoes, season with black pepper and mix well.
Arrange lettuce leaves on plates and spoon the prawn mixture on top.

title: Garlic Ginger Lime Fish Skewers
ingredients: 2 shallots (or half a red onion), finely chopped
2.5cm (about 5g) fresh root ginger, peeled and grated
500g firm white fish fillets (like cod, pollock or haddock), cut into chunks
1 pepper (any colour), deseeded and cut into small chunks
steps: Melt the low-fat spread in a small saucepan over medium heat, add shallots, ginger and garlic. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft but not browned.
Remove the pan from the heat, then add soy sauce, mango chutney, lime zest, lime juice and sweet chilli sauce. Stir until well mixed.
Set the grill to medium-high to preheat. Thread the fish and pepper onto small skewers, then brush with the sauce. (Soak wooden skewers in cold water for 20 minutes beforehand.)
Cook under the grill, brushing with remaining sauce and turning frequently, until golden brown on the outside and cooked through – about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook the rice according to the packet instructions, and mix together the salad ingredients.
When the fish is cooked, serve with the rice and the mixed green salad.

title: Macaroni and Vegetable Slices
ingredients: 1 pepper, any colour, deseeded and chopped
steps: Cook macaroni in boiling water for 10 to 12 minutes until tender. Rinse with cold water and drain thoroughly.
Heat vegetable oil in a non-stick frying pan. Stir-fry pepper for 3 to 4 minutes. Add courgette, peas or sweetcorn and macaroni, and mix well.
Beat eggs and milk together. Stir in cheese and herbs. Season with pepper. Pour into pan and cook over low heat for 4 to 5 minutes. Preheat grill to medium-high.
Place frying pan under grill. Cook 4 to 5 minutes until set and golden. Serve hot, warm, or cold as wedges with salad.

title: Macher Jhol (Bengali Fish Curry)
steps: Put the fish steaks in a bowl with the turmeric powder and a pinch of salt. Mix well and set aside for later.
In a blender or mixing bowl, blend the tomatoes, garlic, 3 green chillies, powdered mustard, pinch of salt and 120ml water to a smooth paste. Keep aside.
Heat 2 tablespoons mustard oil in a pan. Add the kalonji seeds and let sizzle for about 10 seconds, then add the blended tomato mixture. Turn the heat down to low, and stir for 8 minutes or so.
Once the mixture has got a deeper, darker colour, add 350ml of water, stir well and bring to a boil. Once it's boiling, turn the heat back down to simmer on low for another 10 minutes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of mustard oil in a pan, and then fry the marinated fish steaks until golden brown on both sides. Add the fish to the tomato mixture and leave to simmer for 2 or 3 minutes.
Top with chopped coriander leaves and serve hot.

title: Maraq Fahfah (Somali Soup)
ingredients: 300g lean stewing lamb, cut into medium pieces
350g white potato, scrubbed and cut into small cubes
130g brown basmati rice, washed and steamed (optional)
steps: Add the lamb, onions, garlic and stock cube to a large pot. Cover with the water, and cook on medium-low heat for 40 minutes or until the meat is tender.
Add the potato, carrot, parsnip, cabbage and tomatoes, and cook over a medium heat for 15 minutes or so, until the potatoes and other vegetables are tender.
Lower the heat, then add the chilli and dried coriander. Stir well to mix, and simmer for a further 5 minutes.
Finally, stir in the fresh coriander, lemon juice and black pepper, and simmer for another couple of minutes. Ladle into bowls for a nourishing light lunch.

title: Masala Curry Potatoes
ingredients: 350g salad potatoes, scrubbed and cut into 2cm cubes
steps: Heat the oil in a saucepan, then add the cumin, ground coriander and onion. Fry on a medium heat for about 5 minutes, stirring often to prevent the spices from sticking.
Add the chilli and potatoes, season with pepper to taste, and cook for a few more minutes.
Add the water, cover the pan and cook on high for 8 to 10 minutes.
Tip in the tomatoes and cook uncovered for another 5 to 7 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender.
Remove from the heat and stir in the fresh coriander just before serving.

title: Mashed Avocado Cheese on Toast
steps: Toast the slices of bread.
Peel, stone and mash the avocados with a fork. Spread evenly over toast and arrange tomatoes on top. Season with black pepper.
Sprinkle cheese on top and grill for 2 to 3 minutes until melted and bubbling.

title: Vegetable Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 2 heads of broccoli (about 800g), chopped into florets
0.5 teaspoon fresh grated nutmeg or ground cumin
steps: Cook pasta per packet instructions (about 10 minutes); add half the broccoli 5 minutes before completion. Drain and transfer to a casserole dish.
Melt the spread over medium heat, add flour and whisk into a paste. Gradually add milk while whisking until smooth.
Bring mixture to the boil, add mustard, spinach, peas and remaining broccoli. Simmer for 5 to 10 minutes. Preheat oven to 200C (180C fan, gas mark 6).
Pour sauce over pasta mixture and spread evenly. Top with breadcrumbs if desired. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until golden and bubbling. Cool 5 minutes before serving.

title: Meat-Free Cottage Pie
ingredients: 500g butternut squash, peeled and cut into chunks
steps: Cook potatoes and butternut squash in boiling water until tender, approximately 20 minutes.

Heat oil and gently fry onion, garlic and carrots for 2 to 3 minutes. Add vegetarian mince, tomatoes and stock. Stir in curry powder, mushrooms and courgette. Boil then simmer for 15 to 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with pepper.
Preheat grill and warm a baking dish. Drain and mash potatoes and squash, seasoning with pepper.
Blend cornflour with a little water and add to the mince mixture, stirring until thickened. Transfer to the baking dish, top with mash. Grill for 8 to 10 minutes until browned. Serve.

title: Mediterranean Vegetable Sandwiches
ingredients: 1 pepper, any colour, deseeded and chopped
2 teaspoons fresh rosemary, chopped (dried is also fine)
100g skinless and boneless roast chicken breasts, sliced
steps: Preheat the oven to 200C (180C fan, gas mark 6).
Put the vegetables on to a baking sheet and sprinkle with the oil. Add the rosemary and season with black pepper. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.

To make the sandwiches, spread each slice of bread with hummus, then top 4 slices with the chicken. Share the cooled vegetables between them and add some salad leaves. Top with the remaining slices of bread, cut in half and serve.

title: Mini Apple and Banana Muffins
ingredients: 1 medium apple, peeled, cored and chopped
steps: Preheat oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6). Insert 12 paper cases into a bun tray.
Sift flour and baking powder into a bowl, stir in sugar.
Combine milk, egg, vanilla extract and melted spread in a jug and whisk together. Add to dry ingredients with apple and banana. Mix until just combined, avoiding overmixing.
Distribute mixture into paper cases and bake for 20 to 25 minutes until risen and golden. Cool on a wire rack.

title: Mummies' Faces Halloween Pizza
ingredients: 3 slices lean turkey ham, cut into thin strips
60g reduced-fat hard cheese, cut into thin strips
steps: Preheat the grill.
Use a large biscuit cutter to cut out circles from the slices of bread. Toast these lightly on both sides.
Spread 1 teaspoon of tomato purée onto each piece of toast, then sprinkle with herbs. Overlap the strips of turkey ham and cheese on top to look like bandages, leaving a small space for the eyes.
Grill for 2 to 3 minutes to melt the cheese.
Put the halved tomatoes and sweetcorn on top of the faces to look like eyes. Eat while hot!

title: Mushrooms on Toast
ingredients: 4 handfuls cup or button mushrooms, washed and sliced
steps: Put the mushrooms and milk into a saucepan and heat until just boiling. Reduce the heat and simmer gently for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile toast the bread and spread each slice with lower-fat spread.
Mix the cornflour with 2 tablespoons of cold water, then stir the mixture into the mushrooms. Heat until it thickens and cook gently for 1 minute.
Divide the mushrooms evenly between the slices of toast and serve straight away.

title: No-Pastry Mini Mushroom Quiches
ingredients: 1 teaspoon lower-fat spread, to grease tin
quarter of an iceberg lettuce, shredded
a few stems of fresh chives, chopped
steps: Grease 4 holes of a muffin tray. Cut 4 circles of baking paper and place in the bottom of each hole.
Heat the oil in a small frying pan, add the onions and cook for a couple of minutes until softened, then add the mushrooms and cook for a further few minutes. Transfer to the muffin tray.
Mix the eggs with the milk, black pepper and nutmeg. Pour into the muffin tray and sprinkle on the cheese.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180C (160C fan, gas mark 4) for 15 minutes until risen and golden.
Leave in the tin to cool for 10 minutes. Remove and serve with the green salad.

title: Nutty Apple and Celery Rice Cakes
ingredients: 1 small red apple, quartered and cored
1 small celery stick, sliced into 4 pieces
steps: Spread each rice cake with a quarter of the soft cheese.
Cut each apple quarter into 3 slices and place on the rice cakes.
Position the celery on each rice cake for the noses, with a hazelnut on each side for the nostrils. Place the halved grapes under the eyebrows to look like eyes.

title: Oat Fufu with Kale Riro
ingredients: 100g yellow bell pepper, deseeded and chopped
100g red bell pepper, deseeded and chopped
steps: Add the chopped tomatoes, hot pepper, onion, curry powder and crumbled seasoning cube to a medium pot. Cook on medium heat, stirring continuously for 5 minutes.
Stir in the garden egg, yellow and red peppers and locust beans. Cover and cook for 8 to 10 minutes on medium heat.
Add the prawns and kale, sprinkle in the dried crayfish. Stir well, cover and steam on low heat for 7 minutes.
For the fufu, add the porridge oats and cold water to a blender. Soak for a minute then blend until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a non-stick pot and cook on medium heat, stirring continuously until thick. Pound for 3 to 4 minutes until thick and stretchy.
Lower the heat, create wells in the mixture, add 150 to 200ml boiling water, cover and cook on low for 5 minutes.
Mix for another 3 to 4 minutes using a pounding motion until soft and sticky. Divide into portions and serve with the kale riro.

title: Orange and Sultana Milk Pudding
steps: Finely grate 1 teaspoon of zest from 1 of the oranges, then squeeze the juice. Slice the second orange into segments, removing all the peel and pith.
Put the milk, ground rice, orange zest and mixed spice into a non-stick saucepan and bring to the boil, stirring constantly until thickened.
Add the orange juice and most of the sultanas, then cook over a very low heat for 1 to 2 minutes, stirring all the time.
Share the pudding between 4 bowls and top with the orange segments and remaining sultanas. Serve at once.

title: Pakistani Saag Aloo
ingredients: 1.5kg baby spinach, washed and finely chopped
1 teaspoon cumin seeds or 1 heaped teaspoon cumin powder
4 large potatoes, washed and cut into small cubes
steps: Heat the oil in a pan. Add the cumin seeds, chopped onion and garlic, and fry until softened and starting to brown.
Add the chopped tomatoes and green chillies and cook for another minute. Turn the heat down, add the cumin powder (if using) and turmeric, mix well and fry for a further minute.
Add the potato, then the spinach a minute or 2 later. Stir well, cover the pan and cook on low for 10 to 15 minutes until the potato is soft, stirring occasionally.
Remove the lid and turn the heat up. Once the excess water has evaporated, turn off the heat and leave to cool for a few minutes.

title: Pancakes with Caramelised Apples
ingredients: 1 eating apple, cored and thinly sliced
finely grated zest and juice of 1 lemon
steps: Put the flour into a large jug or mixing bowl. Add the egg and milk and beat to make a smooth batter.

Heat a pancake pan or non-stick frying pan and add a few drops of vegetable oil. Pour in a quarter of the batter, tilting the pan so it flows evenly. Cook over medium heat for 1 to 2 minutes until set, then flip and cook the other side.
Make 4 pancakes in total, adding a few drops of oil for each one. Keep warm.
Heat the remaining oil in the frying pan and add the apple slices. Cook over medium-high heat for 3 to 4 minutes, turning often.
Sprinkle the sugar over the apples and cook for a few moments until lightly browned. Add the lemon zest and juice, stir to coat, then remove from the heat.
Serve 1 pancake per person, topped with the caramelised apples.

title: Panjabi Dhal
ingredients: 50g red split lentils, washed and drained
170g split mung dhal, washed and drained
fresh coriander, finely chopped, to serve
steps: Put washed mung dhal and red lentils in a large pot with water on low heat. Add turmeric after a few minutes, stir and bring to boil. Simmer partially covered for 15 minutes.
Heat rapeseed oil in a frying pan on medium heat. Fry cumin seeds for 15 seconds, add onions and garlic. Cook until softened and browning, about 5 minutes. Add green chillies and cook 2 minutes.
Add the frying pan mixture to the simmering lentils, stir and bring to medium heat. Boil then turn off heat.
Cool for a few minutes, top with chopped coriander and serve.

title: Panjabi Keema
ingredients: fresh coriander, finely chopped, to serve
steps: Blend the chopped tomatoes, ginger and green chillies together and set aside.
Heat the oil in a non-stick pan on medium heat and cook the chopped onions for 5 minutes until softened and beginning to brown. Add the chopped garlic and cook for a further 3 minutes.
Add the blended tomato mixture along with the turmeric and frozen peas. Stir well and cook for 3 minutes.
Add the turkey mince and remaining spices. Mix well and cook on medium heat until the meat has turned white. Cover the pan, turn heat to low and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove the lid and continue cooking for a few minutes to allow excess water to evaporate. Top with chopped coriander and serve.

title: Pasta Carbonara
ingredients: 4 slices lean back-bacon rashers, cut into pieces
5 spring onions, trimmed and finely chopped
150g lower-fat soft cheese with garlic and herbs
steps: Bring a large saucepan of water to boil. Add pasta and cook 8-12 minutes per packet instructions.
Heat oil in a non-stick frying pan. Add bacon and spring onions, cook 5 minutes stirring often. Remove from heat.
Beat soft cheese and egg in a mixing bowl, then stir in cooked bacon and spring onions. Add milk, half the hard cheese and parsley. Season with pepper.
Drain pasta and return to saucepan. Add egg mixture and heat gently 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly until thickened. Serve with remaining hard cheese.
ingredientNames: soft cheese with garlic and herbs

title: Pasta Salad on Lettuce
ingredients: 75g reduced-fat hard cheese, cut into small cubes
steps: Cook the pasta per packet instructions, drain, rinse with cold water to cool and drain well.
Mix yoghurt and mayonnaise in a salad bowl. Add tomatoes, cucumber and sultanas, then stir in the pasta.
Serve each portion on a lettuce leaf and scatter cheese cubes and spring onions on top.

title: Pea and Spinach Risotto
ingredients: 10 fresh mint, basil or parsley leaves, chopped
steps: Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook onions until soft, stirring regularly. Add garlic and cook for another minute.
Add rice to pan and cook for 2 minutes, then pour in a ladle of stock and keep stirring.
Once each ladle of stock is absorbed, pour in another and continue, stirring regularly, until rice is cooked and all but 200ml of stock is used — about 20 minutes. Turn off heat.
Put spinach and half the peas in a bowl with the remaining stock. Blend or mash until smooth.
Pour the green sauce into the rice along with the remaining peas, herbs and cream cheese. Stir well and return to heat for a couple of minutes to warm through. Serve topped with grated cheese.

title: Pepper and Onion Melts
ingredients: 2 peppers, any colour, halved and sliced
1 small red onion, cut into thin wedges
steps: Preheat the grill. Arrange the peppers on a baking sheet with the red onion. Sprinkle with the olive oil, then grill until soft and lightly browned — about 6 to 8 minutes.

Toast the slices of bread and share the pepper mixture between them. Arrange the mozzarella cheese on top, then return to the grill for 1 to 2 minutes until the cheese begins to melt. Serve with mixed salad leaves.

title: Perfect Pasta and Tomato Sauce
ingredients: 1 garlic clove, crushed or finely chopped
1 tablespoon chopped fresh basil, to garnish
steps: Heat oil in a pan, add onion and cook 3-4 minutes until soft.
Add garlic and cook 1 minute. Add tomatoes, tomato purée and herbs. Season with pepper and simmer 15 minutes.
After 8-10 minutes of simmering, begin cooking spaghetti per packet instructions.
Drain spaghetti and serve with sauce, topped with fresh basil.

title: Pesto Tagliatelle, Peppers and Squash
ingredients: 350g butternut squash, peeled and cut into chunks
1 red pepper, deseeded and cut into chunks
1 yellow pepper, deseeded and cut into chunks
1 medium red onion, peeled and sliced
steps: Preheat oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6). Place butternut squash, peppers and onion in a roasting tin. Add olive oil and toss to coat. Roast 30-35 minutes, turning once, until tender.
About 10 minutes before vegetables are ready, cook tagliatelle in boiling water for 8-10 minutes.
While pasta cooks, use a potato peeler to slice courgette into thin strips. Add to pasta for the last 3 minutes.
Drain pasta and courgette. Return to pan and stir in pesto sauce with roasted vegetables and pan juices.
Season with black pepper. Serve with 1 teaspoon grated cheese per portion.

title: Pigeon Peas Pilau with Carrot Pickle
ingredients: 4 carrots, peeled and cut into strips
steps: Combine rice, pigeon peas, oil and all spices in a large non-stick pan. Cover tightly, heat until boiling, then reduce to medium heat and cook covered for 25 minutes.
Prepare carrot pickle by combining carrots, lime juice and a pinch of red chilli powder.
Stir rice gently, reduce heat to very low and cook for 10 minutes until water is fully absorbed.
Serve with yoghurt and carrot pickle, drizzling with lemon juice if desired.

title: Piri-Piri Pomegranate Popcorn
ingredients: 1 teaspoon piri-piri seasoning or cayenne pepper
steps: Heat vegetable oil in a large heavy-based saucepan with a tight lid. Add popping corn, cover and shake to coat kernels.
Place pan over medium-high heat, shaking occasionally once kernels begin popping. Remove from heat when popping slows.
Cool for 10 minutes, then stir in piri-piri seasoning, pomegranate seeds and black pepper. Divide among 4 bowls.

title: Pizza Crumpets
steps: Preheat the grill. Toast the undersides of the crumpets until crispy — about 2 minutes.
Flip the crumpets over and spread each with 1 tablespoon of tomato purée. Arrange the sliced tomatoes on top and grill for 2 minutes.
Sprinkle the herbs and cheese evenly over the crumpets, season with pepper and grill until the cheese melts and bubbles, about 1 minute more. Serve at once.

title: Pizza Pitta Faces
ingredients: 1 yellow or red pepper, deseeded and sliced
steps: Preheat the grill or oven to 180C (fan 160C, gas mark 4).
Spread 1 teaspoon tomato purée on each pitta bread and sprinkle with dried herbs.
Arrange cucumber slices as eyes, tomato halves as noses, pepper slices as eyebrows and mouths, and grated cheese as hair.
Grill for 4-5 minutes or bake for 8-10 minutes, then cool briefly and serve.

title: Plum and Peach Grills
steps: Preheat the grill. Lightly toast the slices of bread on both sides.
Mix the soft cheese and vanilla extract together, then spread it on the toast. Top with the plums and sliced peaches.
Return to the grill for 3 to 4 minutes.
Top each portion with 1 tablespoon of crème fraîche, sprinkle with a little ground nutmeg and serve.

title: Pork and Garlic Bread Bake
steps: Combine herbs, pepper and flour on a plate; coat pork pieces. Preheat oven to 180C (160C fan, gas mark 4).
Heat oil in a flameproof casserole; add pork, onion, celery and apple; cook 5 minutes, stirring.
Pour in stock, bring to boil, cover and transfer to oven for 75 minutes.
After 70 minutes, blend together lower-fat spread and garlic; spread onto bread slices.
Remove casserole from oven; arrange bread on top (spread-side up); add cheese; bake uncovered 10-15 minutes until golden.
Cool briefly then serve in bowls.

title: Pork with Apples and Celery
ingredients: 750g potatoes, scrubbed and cut into chunks
1 red apple, cored and cut into 1cm-thick slices
400g pork fillet, sliced into chunks and fat trimmed
steps: Cook the potatoes in simmering water until tender, about 20 minutes. Drain and set aside.
