Heat 1 teaspoon oil in a large non-stick frying pan. Add apple slices, cooking 2-3 minutes per side. Add sugar and cook 1-2 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Roll pork pieces in dried mixed herbs. Heat remaining oil in the frying pan. Add pork and cook over medium heat for a few minutes to brown.
Add onion and celery, pour in stock and apple juice. Simmer 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with pepper.
Mash the potatoes and beat in the milk. Serve with the pork and apples.

title: Porridge with Yoghurt and Berries
ingredients: 100g defrosted frozen summer berries or forest fruits
steps: Put porridge oats and water into a non-stick saucepan. Heat and stir until boiling, then lower heat and simmer gently, stirring often, for 5 minutes.
Spoon porridge into serving bowls and top each portion with 2 tablespoons yoghurt. Spoon berries on top and serve.

title: Potato and Poached Egg Hash
ingredients: 400g potatoes, pre-cooked and cut into chunks
steps: Heat vegetable oil in a large non-stick frying pan, add bacon and cook 1-2 minutes. Add potatoes and cook over medium heat for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally until browned.
Add spring onions and tomatoes, cook over low heat for 4-5 minutes while poaching the eggs.
Poach eggs in simmering water for 3-4 minutes until whites are set and yolks are runny.
Distribute potato hash among 4 warm plates, top each with a poached egg, season with pepper and serve.

title: Prawn Jambalaya
ingredients: 2 handfuls cup or button mushrooms, sliced
steps: Cook rice in simmering water for 12-15 minutes or per packet instructions until tender. Drain well.
Heat oil in a large non-stick frying pan. Cook onion and celery 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add green pepper, mushrooms, tomatoes and prawns. Stir-fry for 4 minutes.
Stir in the hot cooked rice, parsley, cajun seasoning and chilli powder. Cook 2 minutes, stirring until heated through.
Check seasoning, add pepper and serve immediately.

title: Pronto Pizza Squares
ingredients: 2 handfuls mixed salad leaves, to serve
steps: Preheat the grill to medium-high.
Place the wraps on a work surface and spread each one with 2 tablespoons of tomato purée, then sprinkle 1 teaspoon mixed herbs over each. Season with black pepper.
Arrange 2 sliced tomatoes over the top of each wrap, then sprinkle with cheese.
Grill each wrap for 3 to 4 minutes until the cheese melts and bubbles.
Serve with mixed salad leaves, garnished with olives and basil.

title: Quorn and Vegetable Stir-Fry
ingredients: 4 spring onions, trimmed and finely chopped
1 pepper, any colour, deseeded and finely chopped
steps: Put the rice on to cook in a large saucepan of simmering water. Brown rice takes 30-35 minutes; white rice takes about 12 minutes.
When the rice has about 8 minutes left, heat the vegetable oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat.
Add the vegetables and frozen Quorn pieces. Stir-fry for 6 to 7 minutes until the vegetables are cooked but still crunchy.
Add the Chinese 5-spice powder, soy sauce and sweet chilli sauce. Season with pepper and stir well.
Drain the rice and serve with the stir-fry.

title: Raspberry and Banana Shakes
steps: Slice bananas into a blender with the frozen raspberries, yoghurt, milk and vanilla extract.
Whizz together for a few seconds until smooth and blended, then pour into 6 glasses and serve immediately.

title: Raspberry, Orange and Apple Refresher
steps: Tip the frozen raspberries into a blender and add the apple juice and orange juice.
Whizz together for a few seconds until smooth and blended, then pour into 4 glasses and serve at once.

title: Roast Dinner
steps: Preheat the oven to 190C (fan 170C, gas mark 5).
Place the chicken in a roasting tin and roast for 90 minutes until cooked through and juices run clear.
Parboil the potatoes for 5 minutes, drain, then place in a separate roasting tin with the vegetable oil. Roast for 60 minutes until golden.
Cook the carrots in boiling water for 10-15 minutes, leeks for 5-10 minutes, and cabbage for 5 minutes. Add frozen peas for the last 3 minutes.
Rest the chicken for 10 minutes after removing from the oven.
Make the gravy by dissolving the granules in boiling water according to packet instructions.
Serve skinless chicken alongside roast potatoes, vegetables and gravy.

title: Roasted Root Veg with Beetroot and Cucumber Dips
ingredients: 250g small carrots, trimmed, scrubbed and halved
250g small parsnips, trimmed, scrubbed and quartered
250g sweet potatoes, scrubbed and thinly sliced
100g tinned cannellini beans, rinsed and drained
steps: Preheat the oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6).
Toss the carrots, parsnips and sweet potatoes with olive oil in a roasting tin. Roast for 20 minutes.
Turn the vegetables, sprinkle with cumin and roast for a further 15 minutes until tender.
For the beetroot dip, blend the beetroot, cannellini beans and 2 tablespoons of yoghurt until smooth. Season with black pepper.
For the cucumber dip, mix the chopped cucumber with 100g yoghurt.
Chill both dips until ready to serve alongside the roasted vegetables.

title: Roasted Veg and Couscous
steps: Preheat the oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 6).
Combine the peppers, courgette, garlic and onion in a roasting tin with the oil. Roast for 25-30 minutes, stirring halfway through.
Place the couscous in a bowl, add the hot stock, stir, cover and leave for 10-15 minutes until all the liquid is absorbed. Fluff with a fork.
Remove the roasting tin from the oven, add the cherry tomatoes and couscous, season with black pepper and mix well.
Return to the oven for 5 minutes to heat through, then serve.

title: Salmon and Broccoli Pasta
ingredients: 2 tablespoons fresh parsley or chives, chopped
steps: Grill the salmon fillets for 8-10 minutes until cooked through.
Cook the wholemeal pasta according to packet instructions. Five minutes before the pasta is ready, add the spring onions and broccoli to the pot.
Drain the pasta and vegetables, placing frozen peas in the colander so the hot water thaws them.
In the same pan, combine the soft cheese and skimmed milk over a low heat to make a sauce.
Add the pasta, vegetables and peas to the sauce. Stir in the lemon zest, herbs and black pepper.
Flake the grilled salmon into chunks and gently fold into the pasta. Serve topped with grated hard cheese.

title: Salmon and Courgette Bagels
steps: Preheat a chargrill pan or grill. Grill the courgette slices for a few minutes each side until tender. Leave to cool.
Lightly toast the cut sides of the bagels, then spread with the soft cheese.
Layer the watercress and flaked salmon onto the bagel bases, top with the grilled courgette slices and season with black pepper.
Close the bagels and serve immediately, or wrap and refrigerate until lunchtime.

title: Salmon and Cucumber Wraps
ingredients: 213g can red or pink salmon, drained
quarter of a cucumber, finely chopped
steps: Mash the drained salmon in a bowl, then mix in the soft cheese and chopped cucumber. Season with black pepper.
Lay the tortillas on a work surface and spread the salmon mixture evenly over each one.
Top with the shredded lettuce and watercress, then squeeze over the lemon juice.
Roll each tortilla up tightly, slice in half and serve, or wrap in foil for later.

title: Salmon and Salad Bagel
ingredients: 1 small can (105g) or half a large can pink salmon
steps: Cut the bagel in half, toast and leave to cool.
Drain the salmon and remove the bones. Mix with the mayonnaise and black pepper.
Spread the salmon mixture on one half of the bagel, add the cucumber and lettuce, then top with the other half.

title: Sausage and Bean Stew
ingredients: 400g chopped tomatoes (fresh or tinned) or passata
410g tin cannellini or butter beans, drained
100g potatoes, peeled and cut into small cubes
2 teaspoons dried basil or dried mixed herbs
steps: Grill the sausages at medium-high heat for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until cooked through.
Heat the oil in a large saucepan and fry the onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes until softened.
Add the chopped tomatoes or passata, beans, potatoes and herbs. Simmer for 15-20 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Slice the cooked sausages and add to the pan. Cook for a further 2-3 minutes to warm through, then serve in bowls.

title: Sausage, Tomato and Butter Bean Bake
ingredients: 2 tins butter beans in water, drained and rinsed
steps: Grill the sausages for 1-2 minutes until sealed and browned. Preheat the oven to 200C (fan 180C, gas mark 4).
Heat the olive oil in a pan, add the onion and sage, and fry on a low heat for 10 minutes until the onion has softened.
Increase the heat to medium-low, add the chopped tomatoes and simmer for 5 minutes until the sauce has reduced. Season with black pepper.
Transfer the mixture to an oven dish, stir in the butter beans and arrange the sausages on top.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until bubbling and the sausages are cooked through.

title: Scrambled Eggs on Mushrooms with Grilled Peppers
steps: Preheat the grill. Arrange the sliced peppers and mushroom caps (gill-side up) on the grill rack and cook for 4-5 minutes, turning once.
Beat the eggs with the milk. Finely chop the mushroom stalks.
Melt the lower-fat spread in a non-stick pan over medium heat. Add the eggs and chopped stalks and scramble gently until just set. Stir in the grilled peppers and cherry tomatoes.
Warm the pitta breads under the grill for 1-2 minutes.
Place a mushroom cap on each plate, spoon over the scrambled egg mixture, season with black pepper and serve with the warmed pittas.
